title: HTTP API Resources, Representations, Errors, and Handler Layers · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs http api resources, representations, errors, and handler layers from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for consistent feed-reader HTTP API after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Design tenant, source, subscription, entry, collection, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions rather than tables.
Separate routing, authority, request admission, application command, repository, representation, and observability with narrow interfaces.
Apply safe and idempotent method semantics, success statuses, preconditions, redirects, and retry guidance consistently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Resource Model · changed case · Review Feed Go Resource Model
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 3582 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource semantics, layered handlers, problem details, cancellation, and response finality; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Feed Go Resource Model without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Resource Model · changed case · Review Feed Go Resource Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design tenant, source, subscription, entry, collection, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions rather than tables. For Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Resource Model · changed case · Review Feed Go Resource Model, keep Feed Go Resource Model connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Propagate the request context through application, database, and downstream work while preventing client disconnect from corrupting durable jobs. Artifact under review: consistent feed-reader HTTP API.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof. This does not satisfy Feed Go Request Cancellation for consistent feed-reader HTTP API.
The record names the assumption, applies Feed Go Request Cancellation, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Feed Go Request Cancellation record says consistent feed-reader HTTP API looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Feed Go Request Cancellation.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Request Cancellation
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go Request Cancellation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 5060 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource semantics, layered handlers, problem details, cancellation, and response finality; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Propagate the request context through application, database, and downstream work while preventing client disconnect from corrupting durable jobs.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Request Cancellation.
steps.why: Retrieving Feed Go Request Cancellation through the concrete consistent feed-reader HTTP API case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Request Cancellation, keep Feed Go Request Cancellation connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Propagate the request context through application, database, and downstream work while preventing client disconnect from corrupting durable jobs.

Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go Request Cancellation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Every disconnect should cancel already-committed ingestion work.
